name: Shalya
description: Shalya, the king of Madra, is a significant character in the Mahabharata. Renowned for his skill in chariot warfare and unwavering adherence to dharma, Shalya initially intended to fight on the Pandava side. However, through a trick played by Duryodhana, he was obligated to fight for the Kauravas. Despite being bound to the Kaurava army, Shalya harbored immense respect and affection for the Pandavas, particularly Yudhishthira. He served as Karna's charioteer, constantly demoralizing him with truthful and often harsh criticisms, subtly undermining Karna's confidence and effectiveness in battle. Though he fought for the Kauravas, his heart was not in it, and he often lamented the tragic circumstances that forced him to oppose the Pandavas. He was known for his honesty and integrity, even when it was detrimental to his own side. Shalya ultimately became the commander-in-chief of the Kaurava army after the fall of Karna, fighting valiantly but knowing defeat was inevitable. He was eventually slain by Yudhishthira. His character embodies the complexities of duty, loyalty, and the tragic consequences of adhering to one's word, even when morally conflicted. He is also a symbol of truth, even when it is painful.
subtitle: King of Madra, Bound by Duty

keyMyths.title: Shalya's Boon
keyMyths.description: Shalya received a boon that allowed him to choose his own time of death. This contributed to his bravery in battle, knowing he had some control over his fate.

keyMyths.title: Deception by Duryodhana
keyMyths.description: Duryodhana tricked Shalya into promising support before learning which side he would fight for. Shalya, bound by his word, was forced to fight for the Kauravas.

keyMyths.title: Charioteer to Karna
keyMyths.description: Shalya served as Karna's charioteer, constantly berating him with truthful criticisms. This psychological warfare weakened Karna's resolve during the crucial battle.

sources.author: Vyasa
sources.description: The primary source for the story of Shalya and the Kurukshetra War.
sources.text: The primary source for the story of Shalya and the Kurukshetra War.
